notes.note: conclusive: -zu; adnominal: -nu; exclamatory: -ne; infinitive: -ni, -zu; gerund: -nito, -zute, -zuni, -zusite; concessive: -nedo; provisional: -neba; conditional: -zupa; nominal: -naku
conclusive: -ki; adnominal: -si; exclamatory: -sika; conditional: -seba, -kyeba; concessive: -sikado; provisional: -sikaba; nominal -siku, -kyeku; stem: -kye-

conclusive: -si; adnominal: -ki; exclamatory: -kyere, -kere; infinitive: -ku; gerund: -kute; conditional: -kyeba, -kupa; concessive: -kyedo, -kyeredo; provisional: -kyeba; -kyereba; nominal: -kyeku; negative nominal: -kyenaku; stem: -kye-
this interpretation follows Vovin (commentary to MYS 20.4387) who proposes that this is an Ainu loan, from Ainu ta ‘this, here’ and -ke directive particle.
function is not known; found only once, embedded in a prohibitive construction in MYS.14.3526
Treating as a derivational morpheme, do not mark in corpus
Treating as a derivational morpheme, do not mark in corpus
Treating as a derivational morpheme, do not mark in corpus
Treating as a derivational morpheme, do not mark in corpus

meanings.meaning: [theme] derives bigrade verbs from adjectives and nouns

notes.note: Treating as a derivational morpheme, do not mark in corpus
Treating as a derivational morpheme, do not mark in corpus
Treating as a derivational morpheme, do not mark in corpus
Treating as a derivational morpheme, do not mark in corpus
Treating as a derivational morpheme, do not mark in corpus
Treating as a derivational morpheme, do not mark in corpus
result of vowel assimilation, presumably: only as wago omop-, wago opokimi etc.

meanings.meaning: (after adnominal form) so that, because, in order to
(after conclusive form) like, as if (gani only)

entry.gloss: material for, candidate for, designated for

meanings.meaning: material for, candidate for, designated for

notes.note: the suffix is thought to be derived from the particle
Usually refers to something very unlikely to happen. Follows nouns, the infinitive (-ku) of adjectives, or copula ni, completing the predicate in the same way as ar-
nwo reflects merger of no and nwo
pa is an EOJ correspondence with pye
yori is in EOJ and/or reflects merger of ywo and yo

entry.gloss: across; on the other side of

notes.note: bound, only found as N2 in compounds; probably related to the NJ directional particle found in northern and southern Japanese.
attaches to 1st person pronoun wa and intimate personal nouns imo, imora, kimi, tuma
used with lower simple numerals (attested with 1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 8) and one example with ipo '500'
used with higher numerals (attested with 30 and 500)
Noun for now, but let's revisit this
itwo reflects merger of to and two
Only attested logographically, so it's not clear if it's arakazime or arakazimye.

meanings.meaning: safflower; lit. "tip-picking flower" because the flowers are picked and the petals used for dyeing.
wind that blows through pine trees
middle notch of a bow; bowstring

notes.note: the interpretation of 'bowstring' follows Vovin's commentary to MYS.1.3 which is the only attestation of this word
JDB says the meaning is unclear; Iwanami kogojiten says it means "thinly; vaguely". Only attested once (MYS 4.543)

meanings.meaning: forcing oneself; forcibly; with great effort
a story, tale forced on somebody else

entry.gloss: day after day; with each day
day after day; with each day

meanings.meaning: lightly and in spots (of snow falling and settling)

entry.gloss: light and scattered falling of snow; lightly fallen and scattered snow or frost

meanings.meaning: light and scattered falling of snow; lightly fallen and scattered snow or frost
lightly and in spots (of snow falling and settling)

entry.gloss: full unto bending under ones own weight

meanings.meaning: full unto bending under one's own weight
giving tinkling sound (of pearls, bells etc.)

entry.gloss: giving tinkling sound of pearls, bells etc

meanings.meaning: giving tinkling sound (of pearls, bells etc.)
easy to do (when attached to the stem of a verb)

notes.note: taputo reflects loss of koo-otsu distinctions
Vovin suggests that this is a Korean borrowing (also borrowed in the form te) explaining an otherwise difficult passage in MYS 19.4170

meanings.meaning: red boat; boat painted with red clay
dawn(ing) while the moon is still visible in the sky

entry.gloss: be a long time ago; last a long time

notes.note: kiyo reflects merger of ywo and yo
kwopisi reflects merger of pwi and pi

meanings.meaning: great man, fine man, brave man

notes.note: Shared meaning with mune/muna- 'chest' is 'hollow, empty'

entry.gloss: be off; have gone off; be past its peak

meanings.meaning: be off; have gone off; be past its peak

notes.note: sapisi is found once, in MYS.3734b (written 左必之); could be a misspelling for sabwisi which would be the expected form; 必 is elsewhere used to write /pi/, never /bi/.
sike, sikye and sigye are EOJ

meanings.meaning: few, a little; small in number or degree

notes.note: Following the hypothesis that it's a (possibly regional) variant of tana 'completely' as it fits well with the context and doublets with the vowel alternation o ~ a vowel are very common

meanings.meaning: fitting (when attached to the stem of a verb)

notes.note: yowa is in NSK.122, but the reading and interpretation of that poem is anybody's guess

this verb entry follows Vovin's convincing interpretation of tayworani in MYS 14.3368 and tayurani in MYS 14.3392 as negative forms of this verb; most other commentators see adverbs in these two words

meanings.meaning: offer prayers for, offer atonement for
have enough, have one's full, be tired of (have too much of)

notes.note: the meaning "person close by" is found in MYS 1.72
when used as reciprocal, api is most frequently found before the main verb
Simplex form abus- first attested in the Ruiju Myōgishō in EMJ (JDB: 39).

meanings.meaning: [MK for Mikasa (based on a pun on mi-kasa)]
[MK for Mikasa (based on a pun on mi-kasa)]

notes.note: This entry is tentative and follows Vovin's commentary on MYS 14.3476a

meanings.meaning: (after an infinitive) go round doing, do here and there

notes.note: Can also mean 'revere, respect' in EMJ - check JDB for OJ attestations

entry.gloss: worship the spirits; serve; hold dear

meanings.meaning: worship the spirits; perform acts of worship
(subject-exalting version of ar-): be [respect], exist [respect], (after an infinitive) [stative]
(subject-exalting version of yuk-, ko-): go [respect], come [respect], (after an infinitive) [progressive]

notes.note: Two logographic but no phonographic attestations after a gerund as an auxiliary verb
Probably not attested, but cf. pln irinwo
This word is only attested logographically; the expected OJ form is "ugwokas-".
This word is only attested logographically; the expected form is "ugwok-".
mudak is EOJ and in compounds; corresponds to EMJ idak- - check JDB for OJ attestations
JDB also includes 'play (a musical instrument)' attested logographically in the Shoku Nihon Kōki (続日本後紀).

meanings.meaning: staker, a person who stakes posts

notes.note: uturwop- probably reflects merger of rwo and ro

entry.gloss: wither at the top or tip

meanings.meaning: wither at the top or tip

entry.gloss: discard and leave till it gets old

meanings.meaning: discard and leave till it gets old

entry.gloss: dew settles; put; leave; leave out, exclude
see off; dispatch; give as a gift

notes.note: this verb could possibly be quadrigrade

meanings.meaning: suddenly think, realise, be reminded of

entry.gloss: roll up, smooth up of hair

meanings.meaning: dance and sing; frolick, have an utagaki

entry.gloss: hang; string along; feel; relate to
hang t, attach; wear, drape; feel deeply about, be moved by; mention, speak about; say, call, cry

meanings.meaning: feel deeply about, be moved by
sink, set, descend (of the sun or moon)
lean over, lean to one side, incline

entry.gloss: combine; foresee, thinkworry about the future

meanings.meaning: (after an infintive) do to each other, at one another

entry.gloss: do to or with each other; exchange

meanings.meaning: do to or with each other

notes.note: JDB suggests that simwi is a suffix that creates verbs (cf. kamusabwi). It's only attestted once: MYS 6.1052

meanings.meaning: be talked about, be heard of, be told of

notes.note: transitive uses of this verb 'let know, tell about', e.g. NSK 82.

entry.gloss: decline; of the night pass, progress, deepen, approach its end

meanings.meaning: (of the night) pass, progress, deepen, approach its end

notes.note: the verb in the compound is originally twop-, with a variant top-, but in kototop- all phonographically written exx. are kototop-, except in one sakimori-uta (MYS.20.4392); both the deverbal noun (L030718b) and the infinitive have rendaku, kotodopi; one phonographically written ex. (kototopanu in MYS.5.812) is written with 騰 which normally is /do/, but is said to be used for /to/ as well in Book 5.
kwopi reflects merger of pwi and pi
kwopi reflects merger of pwi and pi
MEANING of "ko" is unclear; might be like prefix i
forms compounds easily, also seems to be able to incorporate NPs into compounds
kuye is EOJ; kwoe (MYS 18.4116) is either a simle misspelling, or reflects incipient merger of /.e/ and /.ye/.

meanings.meaning: tattoo the corner of the eyes
(after an infinitive) do apart, away from (e.g. tokisake-, misake-)
(after an infinitive) do from/over a distance (e.g. purisake-, twopisake-)

notes.note: sasago is EOJ (cOJ e :: EOJ o)

entry.gloss: pierce; pole a boat; be trapped by a net, web, trap, etc; occupy; light a fire; pour; sew; name; point

meanings.meaning: be trapped (by a net, web, trap, etc.)

entry.gloss: natural phenomena happen; shine; clouds rise; plants grow up

meanings.meaning: speak incomprehensibly (i.e. in a foreign language)

notes.note: Corresponds to EMJ sap(y)edur- which can also mean 'chirp (of birds)' - check JDB for OJ attestations

entry.gloss: expose to the elements; dry; bleach; wash
transfix at every point, constantly pierce

meanings.meaning: transfix at every point, constantly pierce
bend down, push aside, push through

notes.note: sinop- probably reflects merger of nwo and no

meanings.meaning: pass away; lit.: hide in an island

entry.gloss: possess; mark as ones possession by a sign

meanings.meaning: mark as one's possession by a sign

notes.note: JDB suggests su might be some sort of prefix.

entry.gloss: trail or drag the end of a long skirt or dress along the ground behind you

meanings.meaning: trail or drag the end of a long skirt or dress along the ground behind you
(of birds) be many, flock, gather together

notes.note: sodar-eru in BS.2 corresponds to sonape-taru 'be.provided-STAT.ADN' in a variant (poem 1345 in the Shūi wakashū 拾遺和歌 (1005–1007)).

entry.gloss: spoil a person; be spoiled; joke around
begin, do for the first time

meanings.meaning: begin, do for the first time

notes.note: [MEANING unknown; something to do with riding a boat or riding horses, maybe?]

entry.gloss: be on top of each other, seem on top of each other

meanings.meaning: be on top of each other, seem on top of each other
rise, stand up, grow (of a plant)
leave, go out, depart, set out
be talked about, spread (e.g. of rumours, gossip)

notes.note: 'be talked about' sense attested in a pun in MYS.17.3931; JDB also gives 'happen (of natural phenomena)'

Etymology unclear: tana could be the adverb tana (L004058) but it is often written as 'shelf' (L050949); piki is often written as 'pull' (L031474a). Possibly ta- is a prefix (L000042) meaning "at a distance", attached to nabiki.

entry.use: This is found mostly with se- 'do' (tamuke se-), but at least once with the conjectural attached directly (tamuke-mu, MYS 6.970)

notes.note: This is thought to be derived from tamuke 'offering' as 'a place where travellers make oferings to the spirits'

meanings.meaning: be connected with, be joined with

entry.gloss: wrap; pile up to stop flow

notes.note: tutome- probably reflects merger of two and to

entry.gloss: buckle a bow; pulling string with finger or nail

meanings.meaning: buckle (a bow); pulling string with finger or nail

notes.note: twoke- probably reflects merger of two and to
ko/otsu value for "to" is not known
NOTE tosi pa kiparedo in MYS 11.2410 with the same meaning, suggesting that this is perhaps not a compound, but a subject-verb predication.
JDB: tono may be related to tono/tana (L004058) of tanabik
Etymology unclear: tono could be the adverb tana (L004058) see tanabik-; pik- is often written as 'pull' (L031474a). JDB questions whether this is the EOJ form of tanabik

meanings.meaning: light a fire, burn a fire, light a torch

notes.note: nakyek- is considered a 'mis-spelling' for nagek

Many examples of nanor- have 'na' modified by something, so it is marked as the head of a phrasal argument and the text has a note saying "incorporation". To find these, look for examples of 'na' in a phrase followed by nor-

meanings.meaning: win someone's heart, seduce (< lit. 'make lie down' or 'move with emotion')

notes.note: nabik- 'bend, lie down' is often associated with sexual activity and it is therefore likely that the use of nabikas- in MYS.15.3705 reflects a kind of double entendre involving a romantic or sexual meaning preserved in its descendants, なびかす 'seduce' and なびかせる 'win someone's heart'

entry.gloss: sway; lie down; yield; be moved

meanings.meaning: become familiar and habitual; become worn

notes.note: JDB notes that this is only attested as nipopuredo, so it could be nipopwi-, but they decided on LB b/c there are many QD/LB intransitive/transitive pairs.

"pray" meaning attested in the prose of the Nihon Shoki and early EMJ Ruijū Myōgishō and Kogo Shūi (JDB, NKD); inflection based only on stem negwi (JDB); DVB negwi 'priest' also found (NKD, JDB); negi, seemingly with kō-rui /gi/ and so possibly a QD form, attested in Settsu Fudoki (摂津風土記) according to kobun.weblio.jp; "commend" meaning attested in the prose of the Kojiki in section on Emperor Keikō (景行)) and the Bungo Fudoki (豊後国風土記) among others (JDB)

entry.gloss: ride; travel a road; weigh on the heart

meanings.meaning: reach the end/goal; dock (of a boat)

notes.note: this verb is an EOJ form corresponding to panare

entry.gloss: splash water with wings making it misty

meanings.meaning: splash water with wings making it misty

notes.note: is this really "with wings" or pane- 'reject; splash'?

entry.gloss: praise, ornament; source material; runner on an umbrella

notes.note: only attested in KK 100 as pigakeru; JDB lists this verb as pikageru

entry.gloss: pull a boat off course; pull oars until they bend

notes.note: look also in preceding phrase; sometimes pi is modified

entry.gloss: leave the capital and go to the distant countryside

meanings.meaning: leave the capital and go to the distant countryside

entry.gloss: take the wrong one of two directions; two-time, or cheat on; go past something twice

meanings.meaning: take the wrong one of two directions

entry.gloss: buds swell; still be in buds

meanings.meaning: look up, gaze out (over a distance)

notes.note: Unidic and JDB have 振り放く.

JDB suggests originally to do with visual confusion, listing as a compound of ma 'eye' (L050048b) and kap- 'switch' (L030511a) but it could also be related to maga 'bad, unfortunate' (L051395), magar- 'be bent', etc.

entry.gloss: leave somewhere of high status, go somewhere of lower status

meanings.meaning: leave (somewhere of high status), go (somewhere of lower status)

notes.note: maki is found in MYS 18.4116, said to be a mistake for make
seems to be in need of disambiguation from L031649a
ko/otsu distinction for "ro" is not known

entry.gloss: report to someone higher up; govern

meanings.meaning: speak [humble], report to someone higher up

entry.gloss: wash or purify body in or with water

meanings.meaning: wash or purify body in/with water

notes.note: no phonographic attestations, so it is not known whether the first syllable is /mi/ or /mwi/; etymologies with both midu 'water' and mwi 'body' have been suggested, but it is not clear which is better.
Also examples where miya is modified; miya will be in a phrase and there will be a note saying 'incorporation'

meanings.meaning: around an island, circumference of an island

entry.gloss: look across, look out, look over

meanings.meaning: look across, look out, look over
opposite; other side; across from; facing

entry.gloss: love and nurture; take care of

meanings.meaning: pass; end, come to an end

notes.note: kipe- is usually thought to be a compound of ko- 'come' and pe- 'pass' (as reflected in this entry), but cf. both kipar- 'end, come to an end' and kipa 'edge'.
UB variant is only attested once

meanings.meaning: leaving behind the many islands (lit. the many islands disappearing)
hide (someone) in the mountains [a euphemism for being bereaved (by someone)]
send, give (e.g. a message, a gift)
(after an infinitive) do over a long distance (e.g. miyar- 'look across')
(after an infinitive) do completely (e.g. sugusiyar- 'use up (time)')

notes.note: 'comfort, console' could be a reanalysis of of omopi yaru 'dispel one's worries' as 'calm one's thoughts'?

entry.gloss: be touched in passing, brush against

meanings.meaning: be touched in passing, brush against
(after an infinitive) [progressive], do increasingly, do continuously

notes.note: yepi is an EOJ correspondence with yupi

entry.gloss: be in a section of bamboo

meanings.meaning: be in a section of bamboo

entry.gloss: split t; make ones way through

meanings.meaning: split t; make one's way through

entry.gloss: pushing through with the chest; breasts

meanings.meaning: pushing through with the chest; breasts
determine, decide on (a punishment or reward), punish, bestow
rejuvenating water; water which will bring back youth

notes.note: wotwokwosabwi- reflects merger of to and two
conclusive: kosu; imperative: koso; optative: kosene; negative: kose-n-
adnominal: no; infinitive: ni, no; gerund: nite, nito, nisite; semblative: nasu, nosu

meanings.meaning: know how to; be able to

itype.value: indeterminate (quadrigrade, lower bigrade or upper bigrade)

notes.note: Occurs in adnominal form as "tipayaburu"; could be quadrigrade "tipayabur-" or lower-bigrade "tipayabe-" or upper-bigrade "tipayabwi-".

entry.gloss: passive or potential form of sleep

notes.note: This form is only used with the negative
not in JDB as a verb
part of speech assigment is tentative as meaning and parsing are not known
There isn't an entry for this in JDB; commentary relates it to NJ soras- 'bend, warp'.
not in JDB as a verb
JDB has this as takiswonapi, but NKBT has takiswonape; part of speech and meaning are unclear.
not in JDB as a verb

meanings.meaning: speak incomprehensibly (i.e. in a foreign language)

notes.note: probably LB based on modern kogoeru
Probably etymologically i- 'interrogative' + ka '(this) way'; cf. isa, itu, iku
waro is an EOJ form, in 20.4343

entry.gloss: Maro; chap, fella; attached to or forms terms of address to men

meanings.meaning: chap, fella; attached to or forms terms of address to men

notes.note: ado is almost exclusively EOJ, but is also in MYS 15.3639

meanings.meaning: Aso; title, used with personal names

notes.note: Thought to be an abbreviation of a full title asomi, which is hypothesized to be a compound of asa 'morning' + omi 'retainer'
kami reflects merger of mwi and mi and is also EOJ
wagimwo is the shape in Kojiki

entry.gloss: tree top; new growth of a tree

notes.note: imwo is the shape in Kojiki
imu is an EOJ correspondence with imo
mwo is the shape in the Kojiki
mu is in MYS 18.4105b, but the assignment is tentative
upye is an EOJ form that probably reflects merger of /pe/ and /pye/
yado reflects merger of dwo and do

meanings.meaning: Mt. Miwa; literally "hemp-ball shape", which was used as an alternative name for Mt. Miwa

notes.note: Cf. Vovin MYS translation notes to MYS 1.19.

meanings.meaning: the way back, the way home

entry.gloss: for, for the sake of; because of

meanings.meaning: for, for the sake of; because of

entry.gloss: for, for the sake of; because of

meanings.meaning: for, for the sake of; because of

entry.gloss: for, for the sake of; because of

meanings.meaning: for, for the sake of; because of

entry.gloss: for, for the sake of; because of

meanings.meaning: for, for the sake of; because of

entry.gloss: think from the bottom of ones heart

meanings.meaning: think from the bottom of one's heart
[MK for /susu/, because soot (susu) easily appears when you burn (taku) a fire in a small house (puseya)]
[MK for Miwayama, Kaseyama, which are places where spirits were worshipped]

entry.gloss: hem of a garment; foot of a mountain
vicinity of the foot of a mountain

meanings.meaning: vicinity of the foot of a mountain

notes.note: tadoki is probably a spelling mistake

entry.gloss: sake as offering to the spirits

meanings.meaning: sake as offering to the spirits

notes.note: EOJ forms thought to correspond to iswo

entry.gloss: inside, underside; heart, meaning; future, limit

notes.note: siru is the EOJ correspondence of siri
keto is EOJ correspondence for koto

meanings.meaning: bush clover (any flowering plant of genus Lespedeza)
[MK for moribye (L080249) (because of its homophony with mor 'guard')]

notes.note: yanagi reflects merger of gwi and gi

meanings.meaning: Japanese bush warbler, Japanese nightingale (Cettia diphone)
patrinia; golden lace (Patrinia scabiosifolia); scabious patrinia; yellow patrinia
tai (species of reddish-brown Pacific sea bream, Pagrus major)
island in the shape of a hill; mountain (hill) which is surrounded by water and resembles an island
artificial hill placed in a pond in traditional Japanese garden

entry.gloss: Yamashiro-ji; the road to Yamashiro Province

meanings.meaning: Yamashiro-ji; the road to Yamashiro Province

notes.note: Corresponds to Hōjōzu-gata (放生津潟) Lagoon (Nakanishi 1985: 470, Hashimoto 1985: 291 in Vovin 2015: 2019), which is now Fushiki-Toyama Port (伏木富山港)
Corresponds to Hōjōzu-gata (放生津潟) Lagoon (Nakanishi 1985: 470, Hashimoto 1985: 291 in Vovin 2015: 2019), which is now Fushiki-Toyama Port (伏木富山港)
Corresponds to present-day Katakai River (片貝川), which flows through Toyama Prefecture (Nakanishi 1985: 435 in Vovin 2015: 163)

meanings.meaning: quality, character, nature of a mountain

entry.gloss: mountain that looks like a green fence

meanings.meaning: mountain that looks like a green fence

entry.gloss: mountain that looks like a fence

meanings.meaning: mountain that looks like a fence

entry.gloss: place close to the entrance as you have just come out; stretch of low-lying hills

meanings.meaning: place close to the entrance (as you have just come out)

entry.gloss: place close to the entrance as you have just come out

meanings.meaning: place close to the entrance (as you have just come out)

entry.gloss: tongue of an old person which protrudes when they speak

meanings.meaning: tongue of an old person (which protrudes when they speak)

entry.gloss: hill or small cliff facing or overlooking the sea, bluff

meanings.meaning: hill or small cliff facing or overlooking the sea, bluff

notes.note: dvandva (yamakapa is a dvandva compound, whereas yamagapa is 'mountain river')
yamagapa is 'mountain river', whereas yamakapa is a dvandva compound)

meanings.meaning: forgetting grass; grass that makes you forget your worries
half shell (which can make you forget your love)
morning crow, crow in the morning
morning sleep, sleeping in the morning
autumn field, field in the autumn (ripe for harvesting)
wind from home; wind blowing from the the direction of one's home

entry.gloss: rocks in the offing; rocky offing

meanings.meaning: rocks in the offing; rocky offing

notes.note: the one example involves a pun on oki 'breath' L052519

meanings.meaning: category of flowers, things that count as flowers
sound in the night (of strings being plucked)

entry.use: Only found in wa ga sekwo 'my dear (man/boy)' which can be used about a husband or lover, or about a brother or close male friend; mainly, but not exclusively, used by women.

entry.gloss: cut stalks of wild rice; mat made from cut stalks of wild rice

meanings.meaning: mat made from cut stalks of wild rice

entry.gloss: tatami mat woven from rice stalks

meanings.meaning: tatami mat woven from the stalks of Manchurian wild rice
gossip, rumours, reputation rumours, gossip (what others say)
new silkworm cocoons; silkworm cocoons from this year
assigned lot of (grass) clipping, harvest work

entry.gloss: the bushclover when it first comes into bloom

meanings.meaning: the bushclover when it first comes into bloom

entry.gloss: parted hair; young girl with parted hair

meanings.meaning: hairdo typical of young girls (with the hair loose and hanging down)
young girl (with such a hairdo, or of the age typical of such a hairdo)

notes.note: Tunwo no matubara used to be on the coast but is now the inland Tsuto area in Nishinomiya, Hyōgo Prefecture (Nakanishi 1985: 465, Hashimoto 1985: 22 in Vovin)
mana = 形状言 (< 真な?); not in Martin 87
pitapye is EOJ, with only once occurrence

entry.gloss: offering pot; purified pot for offerings

meanings.meaning: offering pot; purified pot for offerings
direction of the capital; towards the capital

entry.gloss: by the beach, next to the beach

meanings.meaning: by the beach, next to the beach

entry.gloss: spear with a small standard banner attached at the top

meanings.meaning: spear with a small standard (banner) attached at the top

entry.gloss: when nobody is there; when nobody is looking

meanings.meaning: when nobody is there; when nobody is looking

notes.note: this is sometimes regarded as a dvandva compound 'tail and feathers'
use of makura kotoba based on the sound similarity of the place name with the verb pate- 'dock' (L031421a) used of boats.

entry.gloss: deep pool by one bank of a river

meanings.meaning: deep pool by one bank of a river

entry.gloss: many feet in length; big, long, tall

meanings.meaning: many feet in length; big, long, tall

notes.note: Probably originated in kundoku of 無事.
also: yama no sakura and yamasakurabana

meanings.meaning: people living along the mountain stream

notes.note: te is often said to be a reduced form of tape L052528

meanings.meaning: ford, shallow part of a body of water where it is possible to cross
my dear (form of address to male familiars)

entry.gloss: sight from behind of leaving for the palace

meanings.meaning: sight from behind of leaving for the palace

entry.gloss: going to the palace; leaving the palace

meanings.meaning: dew at the foot of a mountain
meadow bunting (Emberiza cioides）or black-faced bunting (Emberiza spodocephala
cord made from plant fibre, used in religious rituals
[MK for kapa 'river', for nagaruru namita 'flowing tears']

entry.gloss: pieces of bamboo put together on a string

meanings.meaning: pieces of bamboo put together on a string
Japanese snowbell tree (styrax japonica); same as tisa
going out in the morning, leaving in the morning
going out at night, leaving at night

notes.note: one ex. SEOJ, one from MYS.6

meanings.meaning: dim time (time when you can just about make out 'who is that')
night, in the middle of the night
night, in the middle of the night
road(s) at night; travelling at night
late at night, the middle of the night, the dead of night

entry.gloss: big-lie bird; false or lying bird

meanings.meaning: big-lie bird; false or lying bird

notes.note: etym of kapo unclear; not in Martin

meanings.meaning: windswept place (also as place name and clan name)
bird stuck on a mochi birdtrap
birdtrap (a stick smeared with mochi (rice paste) to which a bird would get stuck); birdlime
fishtrap (tube-shaped, woven from strips of bamboo)
a type of pheasant, mountain bird
mountain camellia; camellia that grows in the mountains
1st month, new year, beginning of spring

entry.gloss: a gift for your family back home from somewhere else, souvenir

meanings.meaning: a gift for your family back home from somewhere else, souvenir

entry.gloss: honorable gate; imperial court, palace; the emperor

notes.note: mikado reflects merger of do and dwo

entry.gloss: meat from the stomach of cows or sheep

meanings.meaning: meat from the stomach of cows or sheep
Highness, Honorable (in names, titles or appelations)

entry.gloss: spirit, soul, grace; dead persons spirit or soul

meanings.meaning: Ajimasa-no-shima (island of many adimasa (areca nut palms))
dark red colour (of the madder plant)
melia azedarach (deciduous tree of the mahogany family)
messenger, used to send messages between two people

entry.gloss: temporarily depositing a dead body in a coffin until the funeral can take place

meanings.meaning: temporarily depositing a dead body in a coffin until the funeral can take place

notes.note: Meaning/usage conventionalized from asipik(w)i no, MK for mountains and the like

meanings.meaning: [unknown, only found in the MK asipik(w)ino]

notes.note: The older form is presumably "asipikwi"; "asipiki" is written in kungana and only attested in the later volumes (JDB). Usually analysed as asi-pikwi, with pikwi perhaps related to pik- ‘pull’ or piku ‘low’. It has been suggested that asi is the “foot” of a mountain, giving “low-footed (mountains)” (Vovin) or “(mountains whose) feet drag on” but the foot of a mountain is suswo ‘hem’ or yamasita in OJ. Alternatively, “making one’s feet drag” or “foot-wearying” (Horton) is supported by the attestation (albeit logographic) of the phrase asi piku ‘having a limp (< “dragging one’s leg”)’ in MYS.2.128 and the logographic rendering of asipikwi as 足引, though this may reflect a folk etymology applied after the original meaning had been lost, and the shape of pikwi would have to correspond to a hypothetical UB verb rather than (QD) pik- itself.

entry.gloss: type of plant used in dyes indigo

meanings.meaning: type of plant used in dyes: indigo?
gray-headed lapwing (Vanellus cinereus) (a type of plover)

notes.note: ato likely reflects merger of two and to
Corresponds to Ao, Himi city, Toyama Prefecture (Nakanishi 1985: 421 in Vovin 2016: 89).
this word is only attested in MYS.14.3541; the analysis here follows Vovin

meanings.meaning: cord used to tie a hakama (formal skirt) to one's legs
type of water plant: yellow floating heart?

notes.note: azirwo is in kungana (白) and reflects merger of /ro/ and /rwo/
azirwo is in kungana (白) and reflects merger of /ro/ and /rwo/

entry.gloss: unit of labor, shift, allotted task

meanings.meaning: unit of labor, shift, allotted task

entry.gloss: parted hair; young girl with parted hair

notes.note: Only found in MYS.14.3399; usually analysed as kari+bane, but Vovin suggests kariba+ne adopted here
it is not clear that pik- 'pull' is really part of this word, but that etymology/composition is usually given

meanings.meaning: resting place in the grass; lying in the grass (of deer)
vicinity; area next to or beside

notes.note: Corresponds to Karadomari, Nishi Ward, Fukuoka (Itō et al. 1981: 314, Nakanishi 1985: 439 in Vovin)

meanings.meaning: a kind of utagaki taking place by the water, or at the side of a bridge

entry.gloss: rope or net made from bark of the mulberry tree

meanings.meaning: rope or net made from bark of the mulberry tree

notes.note: this is thought perhaps to be related to two- 'sharp' L007199

meanings.meaning: Emishi: an ethnic group living in the east of Japan

notes.note: idumwo is the shape in the Kojiki
ima is in 17.4011 and some interpret it as being ima 'now' (L004025) there
Corresponds to present-day Inami (稲美町), Hyōgo Prefecture (Kinoshita 1988: 30 in Vovin 2013: 41)
/ro/ is written with 盧 which can be used for both /ro/ and /rwo/; L080142 on which the MK is supposedly based has /ro/

meanings.meaning: [unknown; rice straw woven to create a wall?]
catch birds (and the like) with a net

notes.note: Corresponds to Minabe (南部町), Wakayama Prefecture (Nakanishi 1985: 425 in Vovin 2020a: 125), containing Iwashiro (岩代) station
Said to have referred to an 'enclosed field', and some suggest it is not a place name, but a common noun of that meaning
Could also be scorched field? In which case would be from L030219a

entry.gloss: shoulder-length hair of a young girl; usually parted in the middle

meanings.meaning: shoulder-length hair (of a young girl; usually parted in the middle)

entry.gloss: leaving and entering, coming and going

meanings.meaning: leaving and entering, coming and going

notes.note: This may be i- 'interrogative' + sa 'that way', morphologically be like ika
is also by some interpreted as holy bamboo', cf. yusasa L052448

meanings.meaning: Ie (only found in Ie-no-shima and Ie-shima)
[unknown; type of tree from the theaceae family]
type of plant; thought to be a grass of the rumex genus
free time, leisure time, spare time
round tail of an arrow; arrow-whistle
paper mulberry tree (thought to be the same tree as taku (L052070))
Imose-no-yama; Mt. Imo and Mt. Se

notes.note: A seashore area on the coast of what is now modern-day Ōsaka, governed by the Ōtomo clan (Vovin 2009: 50)
A harbour in an area corresponding to modern-day Ōsaka (Vovin 2009: 50)

meanings.meaning: Kagami (only found in Kagami-yama and Kagami-no-yama)

notes.note: In Karatsu, Saga - still called Mt. Kagami
Corresponds to present-day Unebi town (畝傍町), Kashihara City, Nara Prefecture (Itō 1983: 126, Nakanishi 1985: 434 in Vovin 2017: 94)
Vovin says this is "present-day Mt. Ōki (大城山), located in Dazaifu town, Ōnoki city of Tsukushi county in Fukuoka prefecture (Itō et al. 1981: 304-305), (Nakanishi 1985: 422)."
Located near Ishibutai Kofun in Asuka
South-east of Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture

In MYS.1.12a corresponds to an island in Nadacho (名田町), Gobō (御坊市), Wakayama (Itō 1983: 65, Nakanishi 1985: 474 in Vovin 2017: 58). MYS.15.3606a and MYS.15.3606b corresponds to Nojimaezaki (野島江崎), Awaji Island
Corresponds to Nojimaezaki (野島江崎), Awaji Island
Located in Nara Park, Nara - now also called Mt. Wakakusa (Nakanishi 1985: 486 in Vovin 2020a: 269)
Muko river (武庫川) is in present-day Hyogo prefecture
Vovin: "located around the estuary of the Muko river (武庫川) in present-day Hyogo prefecture (Omodaka 1983.3: 341)"
Naniwa-no miya seki kōen (難波宮跡公園) located in present-day Hōenzaka (法円坂), East Ward (東区), Osaka (Kinoshita 1988: 130 in Vovin)

meanings.meaning: Yamato-ji; the road to Yamato Province
way to one's own or somebody else's home
Koshi-ji; the road to Koshi Province
Ōmi-ji; the road to Ōmi Province
the moon; personification of the moon (related to Wu Gang from 酉陽雑俎 - man on the moon who is forever cutting down a laurel tree like sisyphus)
the moon; personification of the moon (related to Wu Gang from 酉陽雑俎 - man on the moon who is forever cutting down a laurel tree like sisyphus)
Sasanami-ji; The road to Tsuruga Region

entry.gloss: fenced in area; area inside the fence

meanings.meaning: fenced in area; area inside the fence

notes.note: kamwo is the shape in Kojiki

entry.gloss: probably a kind of kadsura vine

notes.note: emphatic prefix related to ma- 'fully, completely'
It is in number of cases difficult to tell whether this is a place name or a common noun "place where the spirits reside"
From Ainu karinpa 'sakura, sakura bark', per Vovin
"Kanipa is a small plain found at the foothills of the eastern bank of Kizugawa river (木津川) in Kizugawa city (木津川市) in present-day Kyōto prefecture." (Vovin 2013: 233)

meanings.meaning: hemp clothes, clothes made from hemp
hemp clothes, clothes made from hemp; mourning clothes made from hemp
fire to scare off beasts (deer and boars)

notes.note: this is also interpreted as a fire to scare off mosquitos (L090081)

entry.gloss: hut to have a fire to ward off beasts

meanings.meaning: hut to have a fire to ward off beasts
Kara (only found in Kara-saki and Kara-no-saki)

notes.note: Thought to be a changed form of karausu (L080248)

entry.gloss: small boat making its way through reeds

meanings.meaning: small boat making its way through reeds

notes.note: Used as an image of obstacles
May correspond to Kashii (香椎) in Fukuoka

meanings.meaning: dregs, lees (yeast deposits, sediment from sake making)

notes.note: Corresponds to present-day Kasugayama Primeval Forest and Kasuga Shrine in Nara (Nakanishi 1985: 435 in Vovin 2018: 166)
kimwo is the shape in the Kojiki
Located in present-day Asuka (Nakanishi 1985: 416 in Vovin).

meanings.meaning: mat (made from straw or leaves of the rice plant)
speak words noisily and unintelligibly (i.e. in a foreign language)
[unknown; only in kubututi, kubututui; the 'head' of a weapon?]

entry.gloss: tube, pibe; whistle made from a tube

meanings.meaning: tube, pibe; whistle made from a tube
Kudara, an area in Nara, although originally the name of the Korean state Paekche
(hand-) bag (made from kugu, a kind of sedge)
the state of the land, area

notes.note: said to be MK, but even the shape of the word is disputed; only one attestation
wakakupe has been suggested (NKBT vol.7, supplementary note, p. 492) to be a contraction of waka-ki upe (L050029a), meaning 'time, point of being young'
wakakape is thought possibly to be a corrupted form of wakakupe (L080151)

meanings.meaning: kudzu; Japanese arrowroot (a type of vine)

notes.note: kyenwo reflects merger of, or lack of distinction between, ke and kye

meanings.meaning: colourful, with deep colours in spots
(a kind of) splendid and great arrow

notes.note: Vovin states this may be either "Takachiho peak (高千穂峰) of Kirishima mountain (霧島山) in Nishimorokata county (西諸県郡) of present-day Miyazaki prefecture" or "a mountain in Takachiho town (高千穂町) in Nishiusuki county (西臼杵郡) in the same prefecture (Nakanishi 1985: 457)."

meanings.meaning: mayumi tree (belonging to the staff-tree family)

notes.note: It is not clear whether the tree is named after its use to make bows (ma-'pure, outstanding' (L000062), yumi 'bow' (L052437)), or whether the bow is named after the tree and the writing (and meaning) ma- + yumi simply is a folk etymology.
ne here, as in a number of other words, contributes little to no meaning

entry.gloss: stone or tiled floor made to drain rainwater

meanings.meaning: stone or tiled floor made to drain rainwater

notes.note: Corresponds to the eastern half of modern-day Aichi Prefecture.

meanings.meaning: child of a god, an emperor/empress, or a high noble

entry.gloss: place to which the gods descend

meanings.meaning: place to which the gods descend

notes.note: It is in number of cases difficult to know whether it is the common noun (L051458) or the place name Mimoro (L080132)

meanings.meaning: harbor wind; wind in the harbor

entry.gloss: place for worshipping the spirits; used about the Shrine of Ise

meanings.meaning: place for worshipping the spirits; used about the Shrine of Ise

notes.note: Vovin states that "Minume is located in present-day Kobe city near Minume shrine (Nakanishi 1985: 488), (Yoshii 1988: 73)."
Also called Mutsu or Ōshū Province - corresponds to Eastern Tōhoku
A harbour in an area corresponding to modern-day Ōsaka (Vovin 2009: 50)
Written logographically, but thought to be mwi because of the likely connection to mwi 'snake' (L052526)

entry.gloss: wood used for building a palace

meanings.meaning: wood used for building a palace

notes.note: Vovin suggests it means "everyone" and is a regional form of mwina, which seems likely since it appears as "pito mone" and "pito mwina" is very common, and because mwina comes from a root *mɨr which could easily give /o/ instead of /wi/ in a compound

entry.gloss: place to which the gods descend

meanings.meaning: place to which the gods descend
[unknown; possibly 'you guys' or personal name Morochi]

entry.gloss: low mountain at the foot of a higher mountain

meanings.meaning: low mountain at the foot of a higher mountain

meanings.meaning: an item of clothing worn while travelling to protect the legs

entry.gloss: when everyone acts according according to their own whims

meanings.meaning: when everyone acts according according to their own whims

meanings.meaning: village of nothingness; untainted and natural place
purple, violet; colour dyed from the root of the plant

notes.note: Corresponds to the now-dissolved Nei district (婦負) (Nakanishi 1985: 492 in Vovin 2015: 188)., now part of modern-day Toyama City

meanings.meaning: pink, carnation (flower of the Caryophyllaceae family)
[unknown; for a long time? with outstretched legs?]

meanings.meaning: things left by a retreating tide

notes.note: Corresponds to the seashore between Fushiki (伏木), Takaoka and Hojōzu (放生津), Imizu (Hashimoto 1985: 115 in Vovin)

meanings.meaning: Nakosi (NB: (also construed as) pun on 'na kwosi' "don't cross")
you my dear; dearie (friendly/intimate term of address to young women)
gulfweed, sargassum; a kind of floating seaweed

notes.note: often used as a pun because of the homonymy with na nori so 'don't tell, say'

meanings.meaning: kudzu; Japanese arrowroot (a type of vine)
newly cleared road, path, or field
brocade; cloth woven with multi-coloured yarn
tall azalea which grows in rocky terrain

entry.gloss: tribute of the first crop of the season

meanings.meaning: tribute of the first crop of the season

notes.note: no (in MYS 14.3352) is thought to be a misspelling, rather than reflecting early merger of nwo and no or representing an EOJ form

meanings.meaning: true feelings, deep in one's heart

notes.note: wa is used in the sense 'outline'

entry.gloss: side-sword; sword worn in the belt

meanings.meaning: side-sword; sword worn in the belt
beach pine; pine at the beach

entry.gloss: name of a flower that grows along the waterside; not clear which flower

meanings.meaning: name of a flower that grows along the waterside; not clear which flower
[unknown; name of a red flower which blooms in early summer]
a thousand generations, many generations, a very long time
peasant; person working in the field (?)
[unknown; likens dressing up to the actions of a seabird]

entry.gloss: horse used by government officals along the official major roads

meanings.meaning: horse used by government officals along the official major roads
[unknown; only found in pimekabura; but cf. pime 'haw finch']

notes.note: Corresponds to modern-day Himi (氷見市), Toyama Prefecture (Nakanishi 1985: 478 in Vovin 2015: 183).

meanings.meaning: (evergreen) trees planted at a shrine to celebrate the spirits
sunset; traditionally regarded as a MK, but we follow Vovin in commentary on 14.3402

entry.gloss: grove of cypress trees; plain with cypress trees

meanings.meaning: grove of cypress trees; plain with cypress trees
flat rapids (one occurrence has the double-entendre 'ordinary lover')
hiro, a unit of measure about 1.36 - 1.8 metres long (4 shaku 4 sun - 6 shaku)
Hirume, Hirumi (a separate name for Amaterasu Ōmikami)
cloth made exclusively from hemp (without mixing in other materials)

entry.gloss: small carp which lives in seaweed

meanings.meaning: small carp which lives in seaweed

entry.gloss: layered cloth the same color front and back

meanings.meaning: layered cloth the same color front and back

entry.gloss: field where the rice is in ear

meanings.meaning: field where the rice is in ear

entry.gloss: ripe ears of rice hanging down to one side

meanings.meaning: ripe ears of rice hanging down to one side

entry.gloss: in the middle of the fire

meanings.meaning: peak, height, best of something; something very good

notes.note: Present-day Takadono town (高殿町), Kashihara City, Nara (Itō 1983: 118, Nakanishi 1985: 481 in Vovin 2017: 90, 130)

meanings.meaning: bonds (for tying a horse's legs), hobble

notes.note: Corresponds to present-day Mt. Futagami in Takaoka, Toyama Prefecture (Vovin 2015: 88)
Used about Tamatsushima because it could look like a mountain from afar
Corresponds to Tonami (砺波市) and Oyabe (小矢部市), Toyama Prefecture. Mt Tonami (砺波山) is by Isuguri, Oyabe, next to Kurikara mountain pass (Nakanishi 1985: 466 in Vovin 2015: 176).
May correspond to Saida (西田), Takaoka, in which case Sakita river may be modern Imizu river (Nakanishi 1985: 448 in Vovin 2018: 51).

meanings.meaning: speak noisily and unintelligibly (i.e. in a foreign language)
wave in or approaching a bay
type of arrow used for hunting

entry.gloss: plain with a sign forbidding entry

meanings.meaning: plain with a sign forbidding entry
type of water fowl; little grebe?

notes.note: Shinano Province corresponds to Nagano prefecture (Nakanishi 1985: 452 in Vovin 2012: 32). Shinanwo shore is likely near Fushiki-Toyama Port (Omodaka 1984.17:

entry.gloss: Shinano-ji; the road to Shinanwo Province

meanings.meaning: Shinano-ji; the road to Shinanwo Province

entry.gloss: a type of arrow, apparently named after the feather used

meanings.meaning: a type of arrow, apparently named after the feather used

notes.note: The Old Korean name was Sïra-pïr where pïr means 'fortress, city state', hence OJ Sira-kwi is a translation of that; cf. Vovin's commentary on MYS 15.3587.

entry.gloss: a type of sedge tree, perhaps with white leaves

meanings.meaning: a type of sedge tree, perhaps with white leaves
measure of area of agricultural land
trunk of a bare tree (from which the leaves have fallen)

notes.note: Corresponds to part of Suma district in Kōbe (Hashimoto 1985: 87 in Vovin).

meanings.meaning: a kind of peach which is small and furry (hairy)

notes.note: sora probably reflects merger of swo and so

meanings.meaning: [tall? high? prominent? only in ariwo (L080266) and arine (L050840)]

entry.gloss: market located in a high place

meanings.meaning: market located in a high place

entry.gloss: rope made from the bark of the mulberry tree

meanings.meaning: rope made from the bark of the mulberry tree
stick made from catalpa tree carried by a messenger; bejewelled (messenger) stick
Platycladus orientalis (a conifer which has no difference between the front and back of branches and leaves)

entry.gloss: something inlaid with jewels, decorated with jewels

meanings.meaning: something inlaid with jewels, decorated with jewels

notes.note: opaque, but usually interpreted as tana (L004058) 'completely' + yupi (L031850a) 'binding', so promise

entry.gloss: tatami mat woven from the stalks of Manchurian wild rice

meanings.meaning: tatami mat woven from the stalks of Manchurian wild rice

notes.note: Thought to have originally meant 'with shields lined up side by side' used in a phrase tatanamete i- (L030222a) 'to shoot from behind a wall of shields' and then applied to placenames starting with /i/ by phonetic association.]
Old name for Izumi Province, now the coastal part of Osaka (Nakanishi 1985: 462-463 in Vovin)

meanings.meaning: regular wind (blowing for example when the tide comes)

notes.note: one spelling (跡津) looks like totu, but 跡 was originally used for two

entry.gloss: viola arcuata Blume aka Viola verecunda A Gray

meanings.meaning: viola arcuata Blume (aka Viola verecunda A. Gray)
ritual cord made from the bark of the paper mulberry tree
mat made from the bark of the paper mulberry tree, used as offering; shape unknown
[unknown; possibly a pin on a "yao2lu3" oar used as a handle]

notes.note: It is in number of cases difficult to know whether it is the common noun (L051458) or the place name Mimoro (L080132)
It is not clear whether this really is a different word from tukuywomi L080015
It is not clear whether this really is a different word from tukuyomi L052194

meanings.meaning: [unknown; name of a plant, perhaps pasqueflower (Vovin in commentary on 14.3508)]

entry.gloss: bedroom for husband and wife; house for husband and wife

meanings.meaning: sword with a straight blade and usually double-edged
long buried or submersed tree or tree trunk which has carbonized or fossilized

notes.note: In most cases corresponds to the coastal area below Mt. Rokkō (Rokkō-san, 六甲山) in Kobe (Nakanishi 1985: 429-430 in Vovin). Unapi river may be present-day Unami river (宇波川), Himi city, Toyama Prefecture (Nakanishi 1985: 430, Hashimoto 1985: 218) but Omodaka (1984.17: 164-165) doubts this (Vovin 2015: 149).

entry.gloss: hair hanging down by the nape of the neck

meanings.meaning: hair hanging down by the nape of the neck

entry.gloss: hemp which has been struck to make it softer

meanings.meaning: hemp which has been struck to make it softer
cloth made from paper mulberry bark
cloth strip, hung as an offering or prayer to the spirits
eleocharis kuroguwai (a type of sedge); kuroguwai

entry.gloss: hemp spinning, one who spins hemp

meanings.meaning: hemp spinning, one who spins hemp
sound of an axe cutting a tree
lie (this follows Vovin, commentary to MYS 14.3521)
the year before last; two years ago

notes.note: EOJ; said to be an EOJ correspondence with yeda 'branch'

meanings.meaning: five-hundred siro; large area of agricultural field; big field
[MK for the place name Idumwo (Izumo); thought perhaps to be a corruption of yakumwotatu]
quiet, slow flow of a river; waters of a river
clouds in the horizon (especially to the east at dawn)
the underworld; the world of the dead

entry.gloss: white mulberry bark looking like flowers

meanings.meaning: white mulberry bark looking like flowers
evening wave(s), wave(s) in the evening
plover which flies over the waves in the evening
my dear (form of address to male familiars)
holy seeds (used for praying for a plentiful crop)
cluster of sacred rocks; many sacred rocks together
sake lees soup, kasujiru, dregs or lees from sake mixed with hot water

entry.gloss: sake prepared for someone who is awaited

meanings.meaning: sake prepared for someone who is awaited

notes.note: pye is EOJ correspondence for pa

meanings.meaning: (wanting something to happen) soon, (wondering/not knowing) when, eagerly, impatiently
growing bamboo? piercing bamboo? [MK for opomiya, opomiyapito, mikwo, kimi, toneri]

notes.note: Usually thought to be sasudake, although that makes analysis even harder

meanings.meaning: like dew and frost, dew and frost GEN, [MK for samu- 'cold', ok- 'settle', aki 'autumn', op- 'cover', sugwi- 'pass away', ke- 'disappear', etc]
Papitukwi/Haitsuki (written 延槻, i.e. pap- 'stretch' + tukwi 'zelkova tree'), corresponding to modern-day Hayatsuki river (早月川)

notes.note: Not in JDB. Vovin suggests it means "asks separately" or "escape (my grief)".

meanings.meaning: "like a tsuga tree"; [MK for iya tugitugi ni]

notes.note: The tsuga tree is evergreen and represents longevity, its use may also be influenced by the phonetic similarity of tuga and tugi

meanings.meaning: "(like) waves out at sea"; [MK for towom- 'curve, swell', kipop- 'compete, sik- 'be frequent', etc]
"of a great ship" [MK for (homophones of) tu 'harbour', watari 'crossing', katori 'helmsman']
"(surely, securely) as a great ship", [MK for tanom- 'rely, trust', omopitanom- 'id.']
"(unsettled) as great ship" [MK for tayutap- 'drift, be unsettled', yukurayukurani 'swaying, feeling unsettled']
"like offshore seaweed"; [MK for nabik- 'sway, lie down, fall ill', nabar- 'be hidden']
"with our pillows side by side"; [MK for tumaya 'bedroom (for husband and wife)']
distant sea, out at sea, offing
cloth made from the bark of the mulberry tree

entry.gloss: be plentiful of plants and growths
plank or board for beckoning or inviting the spirits

meanings.meaning: plank or board for beckoning or inviting the spirits
offering (and tasting) of the first fruits

notes.note: Historical Koshi Province corresponds roughly to modern-day Hokuriku

meanings.meaning: [MK for anayum ("walk like a beach bird")]
[MK for karidi (because of similarity with kar 'harvest')]

entry.gloss: Abe tachibana citrus fruit similar to yuzu

meanings.meaning: Abe tachibana (citrus fruit similar to yuzu)

notes.note: Ami-no-ura in MYS.1.5 is the shore of Sakaide-shi (坂出市) in Kagawa Prefecture, Shikoku (Nakanishi 1985: 420 in Vovin 2017: 28). Ami-no-ura in MYS.1.40 is probably the Obama (小浜) seashore in Mie prefecture facing Tōshi-jima (答志島) (Vovin 2017: 114). The location of Ami-no-ura in MYS.15.3610b is not clear (Nakanishi 1985: 420 in Vovin 2009: 70).
From 'chaotically, this way and that' (literally, "like cut stalks of wild rice").

meanings.meaning: [MK for mi- 'see' (and homophones), ap- 'meet', twogisi 'sharpened', kiywo- 'clear', and ter- 'shine']

notes.note: Thought to be based on nubatama "blackberry-lily seed" which are black; however, this is found in later stages of Japanese, but has no independent attestation in OJ. Nuba is not known; tama is usually thought to be L052078

meanings.meaning: [unknown: probably either (a), trees grow thickly, or (b), be secluded (come to an end)]
[MK for pina; distant, lit. "as far away as heaven"]

notes.note: Alternative interpretation is "far from heaven" but I think that makes less sense; prenasalisation likely the result of an intervening "ni" or "no" in the original expression

meanings.meaning: [MK for tori 'bird', kumo 'cloud', kari 'goose' and the placename Karu which sounds like kari 'goose'; "which fly in the heavens"]
[MK for mipye (three layers), Mipye (L090439), takara (L052068), sawesawe (L050730), ar (L030125a)]
[MK] like a bird in the morning, like a morning bird
morning bird; bird that sings in the morning

notes.note: The older spelling is presumably "asipikwi"; "asipiki" is written in kungana 足引.

meanings.meaning: "jewel-cutting"?; [MK for uti, inoti, ikuyo, wa, iswo]

notes.note: Philippi (1968: 320) says this originated because Uchi was "the ancient seat of a corporation of jewel makers, the Tama-tukuri-be" (玉造部)

meanings.meaning: [MK for ake, putagamiyama, mimoro, asikwi, opop, oku ni omop]
"like (evergreen) pines and oaks, indefinitely", [MK for sakaye]

notes.note: fly to heaven; tam- 'fly' is not attested so this is marked as a simple form.

meanings.meaning: "where they speak words unintelligibly" (i.e. in a foreign language), [MK for foreign placenames: kara (L051219) and kudara (L051326)]
[MK for place names Unakami (L090069), Unai (L052255) and for inoti (L051102); from various plays on string made from pulled summer hemp]
[MK for ne nomwi nak-; sitap-; koto dani twopazu]

notes.note: Corresponds to an area containing the cities of Imizu (射水市) and Himi (氷見市), Toyama Prefecture (Vovin 2015: 140)
Variant of Iki, as in Iki Island, Nagasaki Prefecture
usually treated as a prefix meaning 'fifth month'; glossing here follows Martin 1987 (JLTT, s.v.)

entry.gloss: desirable; which one is thinking of

meanings.meaning: [MK for yupu and mo; possibly means completely white]

notes.note: all three examples are written 白香付 and some editions read this as sirakatuke
mwokwo is the shape in Kojiki
Corresponds to modern-day Mt. Wakakusa in Nara
Uchi in MYS.1.4 is thought to be near Gojō-shi (五條市) in Nara Prefecture and Kita-Uchi Station (北宇智駅) (Nakanishi 1985: 428-429 in Vovin 2017: 22)
Exact location unclear but likely near present-day Toyama and Takaoka cities (Vovin 2018:27-8)

meanings.meaning: difficult to do (when attached to the stem of a verb)

notes.note: no attestation, so the shape kwi is tentatively based on the alternation with ku

meanings.meaning: reason (N no/ga kara ni 'because of N')
[unknown; boat made from a tall tree?]

notes.note: Vovin interprets this as a personal name in MYS 14.3462, but it could also be the adjective mana "lovely"
possibly thus named because silkworms eat leaves of the mulberry tree

meanings.meaning: a kind of seaweed (which grows in the deep)
"calling bird" (unknown, but thought likely to be (little or Himalayan) cuckoo or bush warbler)
a kind of water hyacinth (Monochoria vaginaliz)

notes.note: In Nijofukae, Itoshima, Fukuoka; two different authors call this Fukaye (深江町; Sasaki 1983: 520) and Nijō (二丈町; Nakanishi 1985: 446) but Vovin concludes they are the same place.
lexicalized from the continuative form of ar
Probably corresponds to Jinzū River (神通川) which flows from Gifu Prefecture to Toyama Prefecture (Nakanishi 1985: 428 in Vovin 2015: 194)
Usually said to have an EOJ form semidwo found in MYS.14.3546 but this could theoretically contain se 'shallows, rapids'
Shika Island, Fukuoka (Ito et al. 1981: 328, Nakanishi 1985: 451 in Vovin)

meanings.meaning: [MK for the place names Kataoka-yama and Katasiwa-gawa]

entry.gloss: shallow plain of close-grown, thin bamboo

meanings.meaning: shallow plain of close-grown, thin bamboo

notes.note: Seashore to the north-east of Mt. Futagami, Takaoka (Vovin 2015: 87)

meanings.meaning: artifical hill in a pond in a garden
garden decorated with ponds and/or mounds

notes.note: simwo is the form in the Kojiki
simotukyenwo reflects merger of, or lack of distinction between, ke and kye; etymologically this is kenwo

meanings.meaning: Tsukushi (province in northern Kyushu; also used for Kyushu as a whole)

notes.note: Corresponds to Sugaharacho (菅原町) in Nara (Omodaka 1984.20: 233 in Vovin 2013: 279)

meanings.meaning: Awa (only found in Awa-shima and Awa-no-koshima)

notes.note: Located on the coast to the northeast of Mt. Futagami/southeast of Himi city (Kōnosu 1939: 2908 in Vovin 2015: 149).
Vovin says Takamatwo Field "corresponds to present-day Byakugōji-chō (白毫寺町) and Rokuyaon-chō (鹿野園町) as well as to adjacent areas to the south of Kasuga (春日) in Nara city (Nakanishi 1985: 458)."

meanings.meaning: [MK for noti, taye-, iya toponaga-, sa-ne-]

notes.note: Corresponds to present-day Nishi Park, Fukuoka (Itō et al. 1981: 291, Omodaka 1984.15: 86, Nakanishi 1985: 420 in Vovin)

meanings.meaning: [MK for kamwo/kamo 'wild duck', adipu 'Ajifu', munami 'look at one's chest']
bird of the offing; bird that lives in the offing

notes.note: Cf. ipyetutori (L080476), nipatutori (L080473), nwotutori (L090566), simatutori (L080477), all of which also used as MK.

meanings.meaning: Tsuno; corresponds to an area in Gōtsu (都野津町) in Shimane prefecture in Book 2 and to Nishonomiya (西宮市) in Hyōgo prefecture elsewhere

notes.note: bound, only found as N2 in compounds

meanings.meaning: waves approaching the shore, waves at the shore
[MK for puru, puri; lit. 'as the quail calls']

notes.note: Said to be because the quail sings in old places but it's probably that the call of a quail sounds like "purusi", e.g. https://youtu.be/Ugi4x8wcKMs

meanings.meaning: [MK for mikasa (based on a pun on mi-kasa)]

notes.note: kamitukyenwo reflects merger of, or lack of distinction between, ke and kye
Zidaibetu says that this expression does not quite merit being called a MK

meanings.meaning: [unknown; only in tatikomono; komo thought to be EOJ form corresponding to kamo]

entry.use: tosinowo is used together with naga 'long' (tosinowo nagaku) to mean 'for a long time, for many years'

entry.gloss: far away; far removed in time

meanings.meaning: island bird; bird that lives on islands

notes.note: Cf. ipyetutori (L080476), nipatutori (L080473), nwotutori (L090566), okitutori (L080474), all of which also used as MK.
Cf. nipatutori (L080473), nwotutori (L090566), okitutori (L080474), simatutori (L080477), all of which also used as MK.
Cf. ipyetutori (L080476), nwotutori (L090566), okitutori (L080474), simatutori (L080477), all of which also used as MK.

entry.gloss: shrine for the offering of the first fruits

meanings.meaning: shrine for the offering of the first fruits

notes.note: Traditionally analysed as ara-tama 'gem in the rough'

meanings.meaning: sleeveless robe made from mulberry bark
a bow made of wide-rooted catalpa

notes.note: Cf. ipyetutori (L080476), nipatutori (L080473), okitutori (L080474), simatutori (L080477), all of which also used as MK.

meanings.meaning: wax tree, haze no ki (Toxicodendron succedaneum)

entry.gloss: Hatsuse-ji; road that goes through Hatsuse

meanings.meaning: Hatsuse-ji; road that goes through Hatsuse

entry.gloss: Koseji; Road that goes through Kose

meanings.meaning: Koseji; Road that goes through Kose

entry.gloss: girl in service to the court

meanings.meaning: girl in service to the court
[MK for noti ni apu, toponagaku, tayezu, yukupye nasi]

notes.note: Haya is a region in southern Kyushu

entry.gloss: moon and sun; month and day
sun and moon; days and months
wind from the direction of the sun

meanings.meaning: wind from the direction of the sun

entry.gloss: rumor of a crowd; sense of someones presence
mountain with hinoki cypress growing on it

meanings.meaning: mountain with hinoki cypress growing on it

notes.note: in tabuse and puseya and puseipo
South-west of present-day Himi city and north-west of Mt. Futagami - Fuse Lake disappeared due to land reclamation during the Edo period (Hashimoto 1985: 217 in Vovin 2015: 147).

meanings.meaning: sift in a siewe or net

notes.note: Corresponds to part of Himi City, Toyama Prefecture (Hashimoto 1985: 217 in Vovin 2015: 147)

entry.gloss: river ford or harbor in heaven

meanings.meaning: river ford or harbor in heaven

entry.gloss: boulders lined up to form a bridge

meanings.meaning: boulders lined up to form a bridge

entry.gloss: stones lined up to form a bridge

meanings.meaning: stones lined up to form a bridge

entry.gloss: state of being comparable to other people in general

meanings.meaning: state of being comparable to other people in general

entry.gloss: the appearance of a mountain; mountains lined up in a row

meanings.meaning: mountains lined up in a row

entry.gloss: palace built on a height, or tall palace

meanings.meaning: palace built on a height, or tall palace
